description: Most relevant base data around global warming: the global mean surface temperature increase above the 1850-1900 pre-industrial baseline (2023 and 2024), the atmospheric CO2 concentration with the excess over the pre-industrial level derived by formula and reproduced in the calc-validation block, the annual fossil CO2 emissions, and the year-over-year CO2 growth derived by the system 'increase' formula through the time dimension (therefore not calc-validated). Base words owned by other files (Earth, degree Celsius, parts per million, tonne, year, percent, share) are re-declared name-only so the import does not overwrite their canonical description; the climate-specific words carry a Wikipedia ref plus the cleaned first sentence of the lead as the description.

words.name: global warming
words.description: the long-term rise in Earth's average surface temperature, driven mainly by human greenhouse-gas emissions, measured relative to the 1850-1900 pre-industrial baseline.

words.name: climate change
words.description: the long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns, caused since the 1800s mainly by the burning of fossil fuels.

words.name: CO2
words.description: a colourless greenhouse gas with the formula CO2, the main driver of present-day global warming.

words.name: greenhouse gas
words.description: a gas that absorbs and emits infrared radiation, trapping heat in the atmosphere and warming the surface.

words.name: emission
words.description: the release of greenhouse gases into the atmosphere, chiefly from burning fossil fuels and land-use change.

words.name: radiative forcing
words.description: the change in the balance between incoming and outgoing energy in the atmosphere caused by a climate driver such as a greenhouse gas.

words.name: pre-industrial
words.description: the period before the Industrial Revolution (about 1850-1900), used as the baseline for measuring global warming

words.name: excess
words.description: the amount by which a quantity exceeds a reference or baseline level

triples.name: pre-industrial CO2
triples.description: the atmospheric CO2 concentration before the Industrial Revolution, about 280 parts per million

triples.name: excess CO2
triples.description: the atmospheric CO2 concentration above the pre-industrial baseline

triples.name: excess CO2 share
triples.description: the excess CO2 as a percentage of the pre-industrial level

sources.name: WMO State of the Global Climate 2024
sources.description: World Meteorological Organization, global mean surface temperature above the 1850-1900 pre-industrial baseline: 2023 about 1.45 degree Celsius, 2024 about 1.55 degree Celsius.

sources.name: NOAA Global Monitoring Laboratory 2024
sources.description: US NOAA, Mauna Loa annual mean atmospheric CO2: 2023 about 421 ppm, 2024 about 422.7 ppm; pre-industrial level about 280 ppm.

sources.name: Global Carbon Project 2023
sources.description: Global Carbon Project, fossil CO2 emissions 2023 about 37.4 gigatonnes of CO2.

values.source: WMO State of the Global Climate 2024
WMO State of the Global Climate 2024

formulas.name: this
formulas.description: hardcoded formula to select now, today, this year, ...

formulas.name: next
formulas.description: hardcoded formula to select tomorrow, next week, next year, ...

formulas.name: prior
formulas.description: hardcoded formula to select yesterday, previous week, last year, ...
